rounds.round_name: Round 1: Easy Philippines History Trivia Questions

questions.question: Which country invaded the Philippines 10 hours after Pearl Harbor?
questions.explanation: Japan

questions.question: What is the oldest city in the Philippines?
questions.explanation: Cebu is the oldest city in the Philippines.

questions.question: From which Spanish king do the Philippines takes its name?
questions.explanation: The Philippines was named after Philip II of Spain.

questions.question: Who was the first president of the Philippines?
questions.explanation: Emilio Aguinaldo was the first Philippine President.

questions.question: What was the name of the largest naval battle fought in the Philippines?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Leyte Gulf was a major naval engagement in Philippine history.

questions.question: To which country did Spain cede the Philippines in 1898?
questions.explanation: Spain ceded the Philippines to the United States in 1898.

questions.question: Which president is sometimes referred to as PBBM?
questions.explanation: Bongbong Marcos is currently the President of the Philippines.

questions.question: Which president introduced the Martial law in 1972?
questions.explanation: Ferdinand Marcos declared Martial Law in 1972.

questions.question: During which battle with the warriors of Lapulapu was Ferdinand Magellan killed?
questions.explanation: Magellan was killed during the Battle of Mactan.

questions.question: What is the oldest university in the Philippines?
questions.explanation: UST is one of the oldest and most historic universities in the country.

questions.question: Who was the first female president of the Philippines?
questions.explanation: Corazon Aquino is the first female president of the Philippines.

rounds.round_name: Round 2: Philippines History Multiple Choice Questions

questions.question: In which year was Martial Law declared in the Philippines?
questions.explanation: Martial Law was declared in the Philippines in 1972.

questions.question: How old was Jose Rizal when he was executed?
questions.explanation: Jose Rizal was 36 years old when he was executed.

questions.question: Which of these isn’t a written work of Jose Rizal?
questions.explanation: The written works of Rizal include Noli Me Tangere and El Filibusterismo.

questions.question: In what year did the Philippine Revolution against Spain begin?
questions.explanation: The Philippine Revolution against Spain began in 1896.

questions.question: Which of these wasn’t a President of the Philippines?
questions.explanation: Jose Abad Santos was not a President of the Philippines.

questions.question: In which year did Magellan discover the Philippines?
questions.explanation: Ferdinand Magellan first landed in the Philippines in 1521.

questions.question: One what date was Philippine Independence proclaimed?
questions.explanation: Philippine Independence was proclaimed on June 12, 1898.

questions.question: What is the name of the script widely used in Luzon before the adaptation of the Latin Alphabet?
questions.explanation: Baybayin was the indigenous script used before the adaptation of the Latin Alphabet.

questions.question: In which year were the Gomburza priests executed?
questions.explanation: The Gomburza priests were executed in 1872.

questions.question: Which of these Filipino historical figures didn’t start a rebellion against Spain?
questions.explanation: Kapitan Tiago is not generally recognized as a leader of the major rebellions against Spain.

rounds.round_name: Round 3: Philippines History Quiz – True or False

questions.statement: The current Constitution was created in 1972.
questions.explanation: The current Philippine Constitution was created in 1987.

questions.statement: President Fidel Ramos served as a lieutenant in the Korean War.
questions.explanation: Fidel Ramos served as a lieutenant during the Korean War.

questions.statement: Ninoy Aquino was a correspondent in the Vietnam War.
questions.explanation: He served as a correspondent during the Korean War.

questions.statement: The name Manila came from a flower which grew on the banks of the Pasig River.
questions.explanation: Manila is historically linked to plants and river systems in its area.

questions.statement: The Philippines were under US tutelage for 48 years.
questions.explanation: The period of US administration and tutelage over the Philippines lasted for a significant duration.

questions.statement: Tagalog was declared the basis of the National Language in 1937 by Manuel Quezon.
questions.explanation: Manuel L. Quezon declared Filipino as the national language during his presidency.

questions.statement: The Mendiola Massacre took place in the 70s.
questions.explanation: The Mendiola Massacre primarily occurred in the 1940s.

questions.statement: The first mass in the Philippines was celebrated in Limasawa, Southern Leyte.
questions.explanation: The first recorded Mass in the Philippines is traditionally attributed to Limasawa.

questions.statement: President Ramon Magsaysay died in a car accident.
questions.explanation: President Ramon Magsaysay died in a plane crash.

questions.statement: The Subic and Clark Bases in the Philippines were the largest military installations by the US Armed Forces outside America until they left in 1991.
questions.explanation: Subic and Clark Bases were indeed major US military installations.

rounds.round_name: Round 4: Medium Philippines History Trivia Questions

questions.question: Which American general delivered a famous speech title ‘I Have Returned’ in 1944?
questions.explanation: General Douglas MacArthur delivered this famous speech upon his return to the Philippines.

questions.question: Who was the first president of the Philippine Commonwealth?
questions.explanation: Manuel Quezon was the first President of the Commonwealth of the Philippines.

questions.question: Who was the commander of the Japanese Forces in the Philippines during the campaigns of 1944 and 1945 and who was executed for War Crimes?
questions.explanation: General Tomoyuki Yamashita was executed for war crimes.

questions.question: Who was the politician and leading opposition figure who was assassinated upon his arrival and return to the Philippines in 1983?
questions.explanation: Benigno Aquino was assassinated upon his return to the Philippines in 1983.

questions.question: Who was the Philippine President who underwent impeachment proceedings and was ousted from power in 2001?
questions.explanation: Joseph Estrada faced impeachment proceedings leading to his ouster.

questions.question: Which 2015 movie stars John Arcilla as the leader of the Philippine Revolutionary Army?
questions.explanation: The movie Heneral Luna features Antonio Luna.

questions.question: Which Filipino painter who is known for a piece called the Spolarium?
questions.explanation: Juan Luna painted 'Spoliarium'.

questions.question: Who was the Filipino General who is known as the hero of Tirad Pass?
questions.explanation: Gregorio del Pilar is known as the hero of Tirad Pass.

questions.question: Against which country were the Americans and Filipinos fighting in the battle of Mindanao?
questions.explanation: The conflict involved various groups against the Japanese occupation.

questions.question: In which city did Ferdinand Magellan first land in the Philippines?
questions.explanation: Ferdinand Magellan first landed in Cebu.

rounds.round_name: Round 5: Hard Philippines History Trivia Questions

questions.question: On which island in the Central Philippines was Jose Rizal exiled?
questions.explanation: Rizal was exiled to Dapitan.

questions.question: Which Filipino President accompanied General McArthur on his return to the Philippines?
questions.explanation: Sergio Osmena accompanied MacArthur upon his return.

questions.question: What was the first book published in the Philippines?
questions.explanation: The Doctrina Christiana is considered one of the earliest printed books in the Philippines.

questions.question: Which Filipino hero was known as The Sublime Paralytic?
questions.explanation: Apolinario Mabini is often referred to as the Sublime Paralytic.

questions.question: What was the name given to the first batch of teachers who arrived in the Philippines from the United States?
questions.explanation: The group of American teachers who arrived in the Philippines were known as the Thomasites.

questions.question: Who composed the National Anthem of the Philippines?
questions.explanation: Julian Felipe composed the music for the Philippine National Anthem.

questions.question: Who was the Filipino woman who made the first ever Philippine flag?
questions.explanation: Marcela Mariño Agoncillo is recognized for her role in creating the first Philippine flag.

questions.question: Which Spanish conquistador who founded Manila?
questions.explanation: Miguel López de Legazpi is credited with founding Manila.

questions.question: Who was the famous World War II guerilla fighter who became the leader of Hukbalahap Communist Insurgents during the 40s and 50s?
questions.explanation: Luis Taruc was a significant figure in the communist insurgency.

questions.question: Which Filipino was elected as UN Secretary General in 1949?
questions.explanation: Carlos P. Romulo was elected as the UN Secretary General.
